summary:
## Security Analysis Summary: eclipse__hawkbit_0.3.0m6 ### Overview Eclipse hawkBit is an IoT software update management platform. This analysis focused on Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities in the Vaadin-based management UI (`hawkbit-ui`). Multiple **stored XSS** vulnerabilities were confirmed via CodeQL taint flow analysis, affecting the web-based management console. --- ### Findings #### Finding 1: Stored XSS via `HawkbitCommonUtil` HTML Construction with Unencoded Entity Data (CWE-079, CWE-116) **Severity: High** **Files affected:** - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/utils/HawkbitCommonUtil.java` (lines 117, 136, 154, 273, 282, 303-317) - Multiple callers: `ArtifactDetailsLayout.java`, `AbstractTableDetailsLayout.java`, `SoftwareModuleDetailsTable.java`, `TargetTableHeader.java`, `ActionHistoryLayout.java`, `BaseSwModuleBeanQuery.java`, `SoftwareModuleTable.java` **Description:** `HawkbitCommonUtil` contains many helper methods (e.g., `getFormatedLabel`, `getNameAndVersionFormatted`, `concatStrings`) that build HTML strings using `StringBuilder.append()` with entity data such as `getName()`, `getVersion()`, `getDescription()`, and `getFilename()` retrieved from the database. These methods do **not** HTML-encode the entity values before appending them. The resulting HTML strings are then passed as content to Vaadin UI components (`Label.setValue()`, `Label.setCaption()`, `setDescription()`). Since Vaadin renders these in `ContentMode.HTML`, any HTML/JavaScript in the stored entity name (e.g., ``) executes in the administrator's browser. **Taint path (example):** ``` SoftwareModuleTable.java:175 → getName() → HawkbitCommonUtil.java:263 (concatStrings) → HawkbitCommonUtil.java:117 (append HTML "") → displayed as HTML in Vaadin table ``` --- #### Finding 2: Stored XSS in `DistributionSetDetails` and `AbstractTableDetailsLayout` HTML Label Construction (CWE-079) **Severity: High** **Files affected:** - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/distributions/dstable/DistributionSetDetails.java` (lines 116-148) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/common/detailslayout/AbstractTableDetailsLayout.java` (lines 141-142, 282, 302) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/common/detailslayout/AbstractNamedVersionedEntityTableDetailsLayout.java` (lines 34-35) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/common/detailslayout/SoftwareModuleDetailsTable.java` (lines 229-232) **Description:** The details layout panels for Distribution Sets and Software Modules retrieve entity names, versions, and descriptions from the database and pass them through `HawkbitCommonUtil` HTML builder utilities before setting them as Vaadin Label values via `setValue()` and `setCaption()`. No HTML encoding is applied. An attacker who can create/update a distribution set or software module name/description with XSS payload will have it executed when an administrator views the details panel. --- #### Finding 3: Stored XSS in `DistributionBarHelper` HTML Progress Bars in Rollout View (CWE-079) **Severity: High** **Files affected:** - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/rollout/DistributionBarHelper.java` (lines 96-97) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/rollout/rollout/RolloutListGrid.java` (line 802) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/rollout/rolloutgroup/RolloutGroupListGrid.java` (line 329) **Description:** `DistributionBarHelper.getDistributionBarAsHTMLString()` generates HTML `
` based progress bar representations of rollout status. The method calls `StringBuilder.append()` with `
`, ``, and `` HTML tags. These HTML strings are used in Vaadin grid cells via custom HTML renderers (`HtmlLabelRenderer`). If rollout group names or status labels contain unencoded user-controlled data, these will be executed as HTML. --- #### Finding 4: Stored XSS in Custom Vaadin HTML Renderers (`HtmlLabelRenderer`, `AbstractHtmlLabelConverter`) in Grid Cells (CWE-079) **Severity: High** **Files affected:** - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/customrenderers/renderers/HtmlLabelRenderer.java` - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/customrenderers/renderers/AbstractHtmlLabelConverter.java` - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/artifacts/smtable/SoftwareModuleTable.java` (lines 175-176) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/distributions/dstable/DistributionSetTable.java` (lines 379-380, 423) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/distributions/smtable/SwModuleTable.java` (lines 298-299) - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/common/tagdetails/TagAssignementComboBox.java` (line 125) **Description:** Several Vaadin table and grid components use custom `HtmlLabelRenderer` and `AbstractHtmlLabelConverter` to render HTML content in table cells. Entity names (software module names + versions) are passed to `HawkbitCommonUtil.getFormatedLabel()` / `concatStrings()` which builds HTML strings and these are passed to `addItem()` and `setColumnHeader()` on Vaadin tables. As the item is rendered via the HTML renderer, any XSS payload in a software module or distribution set name will execute in grid cells. **Taint path:** ``` SoftwareModuleTable.java:175 → getName() → HawkbitCommonUtil.concatStrings() → getFormatedLabel() (builds HTML) → addItem() with HtmlLabelRenderer → XSS executed in grid cell ``` --- #### Finding 5: Stored XSS in `AbstractFilterButtons` HTML Tooltips (Tag/Type Filter Buttons) (CWE-079) **Severity: High** **Files affected:** - `hawkbit-ui/src/main/java/org/eclipse/hawkbit/ui/common/filterlayout/AbstractFilterButtons.java` (lines 291-294) - Used by: `TargetTagFilterButtons.java`, `DistributionTagButtons.java`, `SMTypeFilterButtons.java`, `DSTypeFilterButtons.java` **Description:** `AbstractFilterButtons` generates HTML tooltip strings for filter buttons (target tags, distribution set tags, software module types) by appending entity names from the database using `StringBuilder.append()`. The resulting HTML string is used as a Vaadin button description (tooltip), which is rendered as HTML. If a tag name contains `

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HTML string construction with user-controlled data (XSS)
 * @description Building HTML strings with user data from database or external input without encoding
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xss-html-string-construction
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Find methods that build HTML strings using StringBuilder/String concatenation
from MethodCall mc, Method m, StringLiteral htmlTag
where
  // Look for HTML tag construction patterns
  (htmlTag.getValue().matches("<%") or 
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<div%") or 
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<span%") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<a %") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<img%") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<style%") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("</") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<font%") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<label%") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<button%") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<td%") or
   htmlTag.getValue().matches("<tr%"))
  and
  // The HTML literal is part of an append/concat call
  (mc.getMethod().hasName("append") or mc.getMethod().hasName("concat"))
  and mc.getAnArgument() = htmlTag
  and m = mc.getMethod()
select mc, "HTML string construction found - potential XSS if combined with unsanitized user input"
findings.hypothesis: HTML string construction with concatenation that may include user-controlled data

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Stored XSS in DistributionBarHelper - rollout names in HTML bars
 * @description Rollout group names flow into HTML bar generation without encoding
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/stored-xss-distribution-bar
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Find methods in DistributionBarHelper that build HTML
from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  m = mc.getMethod() and
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.eclipse.hawkbit.ui.rollout", "DistributionBarHelper") and
  (m.hasName("getDistributionBarAsHTMLString") or m.hasName("createBarSegment") or m.hasName("appendTooltip"))
select mc, "DistributionBarHelper HTML generation call - check if entity names are HTML-encoded before inclusion"
findings.hypothesis: DistributionBarHelper builds HTML distribution bars using rollout names/data without HTML encoding

message.text: DistributionBarHelper HTML generation call - check if entity names are HTML-encoded before inclusion
DistributionBarHelper HTML generation call - check if entity names are HTML-encoded before inclusion

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Stored XSS in filter button captions and notification messages
 * @description Entity names used in Vaadin button captions or notification HTML without encoding
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/stored-xss-filter-buttons-notifications
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Find calls to setCaption with entity names directly (no encoding)
from MethodCall mc, string className
where
  (
    // AbstractFilterButtons HTML tooltip generation (line ~291)
    mc.getMethod().hasName("append") and
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("org.eclipse.hawkbit.ui.common.filterlayout", "AbstractFilterButtons") and
    className = "AbstractFilterButtons"
  ) or
  (
    // NotificationMessage.setDescription or similar
    (mc.getMethod().hasName("setDescription") or mc.getMethod().hasName("setCaption") or mc.getMethod().hasName("show")) and
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.eclipse.hawkbit.ui.utils", "NotificationMessage") and
    className = "NotificationMessage"
  ) or
  (
    // BulkUploadHandler HTML append
    mc.getMethod().hasName("append") and
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.eclipse.hawkbit.ui.management.targettable", "BulkUploadHandler") and
    className = "BulkUploadHandler"
  ) or
  (
    // TargetBulkUpdateWindowLayout HTML label generation
    mc.getMethod().hasName("append") and
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.eclipse.hawkbit.ui.management.targettable", "TargetBulkUpdateWindowLayout") and
    className = "TargetBulkUpdateWindowLayout"
  )
select mc, "HTML construction in " + className + " - entity data appended to HTML string at this location"
findings.hypothesis: HTML construction in filter buttons, notifications and bulk update handlers with entity names
